title: Botswana Individual Tax Return (BURS Form ITA 20/96)
description: The Botswana Unified Revenue Service's (BURS) "Tax Return and Return of Other Information of an Individual" (Form ITA 20/96), used by an individual taxpayer to declare personal particulars and all sources of income (business, farming, rental, employment, interest, property disposals, other income, and foreign investments), claim exempt income and deductible contributions/premiums, declare tax credits already deducted, answer general information questions, and make the accompanying signed declaration, for the tax year ended 30th June. Opens Botswana as the registry's 80th jurisdiction (Taxes, 1 of 6). This schema does not file the return itself; the live source is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Botswana or the Botswana Unified Revenue Service.

source.documentRef: Form ITA 20/96, "Tax Return and Return of Other Information of an Individual", Botswana Unified Revenue Service, 6 pages (2-page return form plus a 4-page completion guide), native (searchable-text) PDF.

verification.notes:
## Candidate selection

GOV-4307 ("GovSchema Standard Research"). This cycle's own Known Gaps
re-scan found the registry's existing 79-jurisdiction backlog largely
resolved to confirmed dead ends at every near-complete jurisdiction
screened (AE Passport, BR/CH/PL/SK Visa, CZ Passport, GR National ID, NO
Passport — all previously re-confirmed weak/dead-end across 2+ prior
cycles each), so this cycle scouted new-jurisdiction candidates instead,
per the standing routine's own "prefer deepening an open backlog item;
otherwise scout new jurisdictions in parallel" heuristic. Three
candidates were screened in parallel via independent research agents:
Botswana, Uganda, and Bolivia, across all six verticals each. Botswana
came back the strongest by a wide margin — 4 of 6 verticals
(Taxes, Visa, DMV, Passport) confirmed STRONG on first pass, only
Business Formation (CIPA's initial registration is portal-gated; only
post-incorporation maintenance forms are downloadable) and National ID
(Omang, in-person only) dead ends. Uganda came back 3 of 6 STRONG
(Business Formation, National ID, DMV); Bolivia came back weak (only a
JPG-scanned Taxes form, every other vertical a hard SPA/login/timeout
wall). Botswana was picked as the new 80th jurisdiction; its Taxes form
was chosen to author first per the scouting agent's own recommendation
(cleanest unauthenticated 200 response, richest field set, and a
built-in Guide explaining every field) — see the Known Gaps section for
Botswana's other three disclosed-STRONG verticals, left as open backlog
for a future cycle.

## Reaching the live source

Independently re-fetched
`https://www.burs.org.bw/phocadownload/individual%20blank%20return.pdf`
directly (not just trusting the scouting agent's report):

- HTTP 200, 148,353 bytes, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate — a plain
  unauthenticated `curl` with a realistic desktop User-Agent reached it
  cleanly.
- sha256
  `5480c72c7a78cae21ac8942855e5e74fccf04540522d185ebd38d2b3321f9ec8`.
- PDF header `%PDF-1.6` at byte 0 — a genuine native (searchable-text)
  PDF, confirmed by clean `pdfjs-dist` text-layer extraction across all 6
  pages (no OCR/scan-image fallback needed).

## Extraction method

Extracted with `pdfjs-dist` (vendored from an existing scratch install at
`/tmp/node_modules`, CommonJS build at `legacy/build/pdf.js`), reading
each text item's raw string and its `transform` x/y position across all 6
pages. Also rendered pages 1-2 to PNG via `node-canvas`
(`/tmp/node_modules/canvas`) at 2.5x scale to visually confirm table/grid
row structure — this environment's embedded-font glyph outlines did not
rasterize (blank text, grid lines only), the same known limitation
documented in this registry's `zm/zra/individual-income-tax-return`
precedent, so the rendered images were used only to inspect ruled-line
row counts, never as a substitute for the clean text-layer extraction
(which read every field label correctly).

## Document structure

The 6-page PDF is Form ITA 20/96 itself (pages 1-2, footer-unstamped)
followed by its own 4-page "Form ITA 20 (GUIDE) (2011)" completion guide
(pages 3-6). Page 1 covers Section A (Declaration of Personal
Particulars) and the start of Section B (Income): B.1 Business, B.2
Farming. Page 2 continues Section B: B.3 Rental Income, B.4 Employment
(Remuneration and Benefits/Allowances), B.5 Interest (Building Society/
Bank/Other), B.6 Property Disposals, B.7 Other Income (three lettered
rows, a=Pension/Annuity fixed, b/c free), B.8 Income from Foreign
Investments (three rows: Interest, Dividends, Business — for Botswana
citizens only); then Section C (Exempt Income), Section D (Claims:
Pension Fund Contributions, Retirement Annuity Premiums), Section E (Tax
Credits), Section F (General Information, two Yes/No questions), and
Section G (Declaration).

## Scope

Models the full 2-page return (Sections A-G) — unlike this registry's
larger multi-schedule tax-return precedents (e.g. Zambia's 19-page ITF
46_1, Ukraine's DPS declaration with 10 companion schedules), this form's
entire income-declaration structure fits on a single page (page 2) with
no separate business/farming computation schedule to defer, so no
follow-on-schedule scoping exclusion was needed. Every printed section is
modelled; no section is deferred as out-of-scope.

Per the Guide's own instruction ("All numbered parts of [Section B] to be
completed entering the word 'NONE' if you have no income from any
particular source"), every Section B income-category field is modelled
`required: false` — a GovSchema-driven submission's own omission of these
fields is the machine equivalent of the source form's manual "NONE"
convention; this registry does not fabricate a value to represent
absence.

## Disclosed findings and interpretation choices

1. **`residentialAddress` collapses the form's own two alternative
   printed formats into one field** — the source prints "(a) House/Plot
   Number, Name of Street, Name of Town — if you stay in town" and "(b)
   House/Plot Number, Name of Ward, Name of Village — if you stay in a
   village" as two blank-line continuations of the same Sr No 2 item, not
   two separate addresses; there is no printed town/village indicator
   distinguishing two distinct values.
2. **`daysPresentInBotswana`'s own printed sentence repeats "...during
   the year ended 30th June, ...."**, which this schema treats as
   referring to the same tax year already captured in `taxYearEnded`
   (the form's own header blank), not a second, independent year field.
3. **`employmentRemunerationPeriodEmployed` is modelled as free text**,
   not a From/To date pair, since Section B.4(a)'s own column header
   prints only "Period Employed" (unlike Section B.3 Rental Income's
   explicit "Period Let From/To", which this schema does split into two
   `date` fields).
4. **Section B.7 Other Income's three lettered rows (a/b/c) are modelled
   as three distinct field groups**, since the source genuinely prints
   three separate blank rows lettered a/b/c — row a's "Nature of Income"
   is fixed as "Pension/Annuity" per the printed label; rows b/c are
   fully blank/free-text, per the Guide's own instruction to specify the
   nature of any other income "not disclosed in Part 1-6" (excluding
   dividends).
5. **Section B.8 Income from Foreign Investments prints all three rows
   labelled "a."** ("a. Interest", "a. Dividends", "a. Business") rather
   than a/b/c — read as a bullet-style list-marker artifact of the source
   PDF's own layout, not literal repeated sub-item lettering, since each
   row's own "Nature of Income" column value (Interest/Dividends/
   Business) is already distinct and printed. Modelled as three
   independent field groups keyed by that printed nature, consistent with
   Section B.5's Interest a/b/c treatment. No `requiredWhen` gate ties
   these to `countryOfCitizenship`, since the section's own "(for
   Botswana citizens only)" qualifier is prose guidance, not a printed
   boolean/checkbox this schema could condition on, and
   `countryOfCitizenship` is free text rather than a Botswana/non-Botswana
   enum.
6. **`signedByAuthorisedAgent` is a disclosed directly-supplied boolean**,
   modelling the source's own "Taxpayer/Authorised Agent* *Delete as
   appropriate" manual strike-through convention — the same
   no-printed-checkbox pattern this registry's `et/ics/passport-application`
   and `zm/zra/individual-income-tax-return` (Tax Agent block) already use
   for their own analogous fields.
7. **The Section G declaration's own printed "...of..." blank is not
   modelled as a second address field** — read as the same "Address for
   Service of Notice" value already captured in Section A (Sr No 2.b),
   restated within the sworn-declaration sentence's own grammar, not a
   newly-collected value.
8. **Excluded as official-use-only fields, not applicant-supplied data:**
   page 1's "For Office Use Only" block (Issued From/Office Address
   Stamp, Received/Tax Office Date Stamp).

## Conformance

3 valid mock scenarios — `valid-employment-and-interest-income` (an
individual with Section B.4 Employment income from one employer, Section
B.5 Bank interest, and a signed declaration, no business/farming/rental
income populated); `valid-business-and-rental-income` (an individual with
Section B.1 Business income, Section B.3 Rental income, Section D
Pension Fund contributions, and both Section F questions answered "NO");
and `valid-agent-signed-with-gifted-property` (an amended-style return
signed by an authorised agent, exercising `signedByAuthorisedAgent`'s and
both Section F questions' `requiredWhen`-gated fields) — plus 9
mutation-control fixtures (one missing statically-required field from
each of `taxYearEnded`, `fullName`, `residentialAddress`,
`countryOfCitizenship`, `daysPresentInBotswana`,
`usedRentedPropertyInTaxYear`, `movablePropertyTransferredByGift`,
`declarationDate`, `declarationSignature` — 9 fixtures) and one
unknown-field-rejected fixture, committed under
`conformance/bw/burs/individual-tax-return/1.0.0/`.

An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required/
requiredWhen rules directly from this schema's own `fields[]`, discarded
after use, not committed) ran all 13 fixtures: all 3 valid scenarios at 0
errors, all 9 static-required mutation controls each raising exactly 1
error, the unknown-field fixture correctly rejected, and confirmed every
`requiredWhen` field reference resolves to a real field name (0 dangling
references). Validated clean with `node tools/validate.mjs` and
`node tools/validate-ajv.mjs`, individually and as part of the full
registry run.

steps.title: Section A: Declaration of Personal Particulars
Section B.1-2: Business and Farming Income
Section B.3-8: Rental, Employment, Interest, Property Disposal, Other and Foreign Investment Income
Section C-D: Exempt Income and Claims
Section E-F: Tax Credits and General Information

fields.name: taxYearEnded
fields.label: Tax Year Ended 30th June
fields.sourceRef: Page 1 header — "TAX RETURN AND RETURN OF OTHER INFORMATION OF AN INDIVIDUAL FOR TAX YEAR ENDED 30TH JUNE, ...."

fields.name: fullName
fields.label: Full Name
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 1 — "FULL NAME:"

fields.name: residentialAddress
fields.label: Residential Address (not Postal Address)
fields.description: The form prints two alternative blank-line formats for this single address: (a) House/Plot Number, Name of Street, Name of Town — if the filer stays in town; (b) House/Plot Number, Name of Ward, Name of Village — if the filer stays in a village. Modelled as one free-text field covering whichever format applies, since the form provides no separate town/village indicator distinguishing two distinct addresses.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 2.a — "Residential Address (not Postal Address)"

fields.name: addressForServiceOfNotice
fields.label: Address for Service of Notice
fields.description: Optional per the form's own layout: printed as a distinct blank beneath the residential address with no explicit mandatory marker, for filers whose notice address differs from their residential address.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 2.b — "Address for Service of Notice"

fields.name: spouseFullName
fields.label: Full Name of Spouse (Mr/Mrs)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 4 — "Full Name of Spouse (Mr/Mrs)"

fields.name: spousePostalAddress
fields.label: Postal Address of Spouse
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 4 — "Postal Address" (same line as spouse's full name)

fields.name: countryOfCitizenship
fields.label: Country of Citizenship
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 5 — "Country of Citizenship"

fields.name: nationalIdentityCardNumber
fields.label: National Identity Card No.
fields.description: Modelled optional: not every filer (e.g. a non-citizen resident taxpayer) necessarily holds a Botswana National Identity Card.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 5 — "National Identity Card No."

fields.name: daysPresentInBotswana
fields.label: Days present in Botswana during the tax year
fields.description: The form's own printed sentence continues "...during the year ended 30th June, ...." — the same tax year already captured in taxYearEnded, so no separate year field is modelled here.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 6 — "I was present in Botswana for ... days during the year ended 30th June, ..."

fields.name: lastReturnYearEnded
fields.label: Year ended 30th June of last Botswana Tax Return lodged
fields.description: Left blank for a filer's first-ever Botswana tax return.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 7 — "My last Botswana Tax Return for year ended 30th June ... was lodged in the ... Tax Office"

fields.name: lastReturnTaxOffice
fields.label: Tax Office where last return was lodged
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section A, Sr No 7 — "...was lodged in the ... Tax Office"

fields.name: businessNameAndAddress
fields.label: Name and Address of Business
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section B.1 BUSINESS (Trade, Profession and Location) — "Name and Address of Business"

fields.name: farmingLocationOfFarm
fields.label: Location of Farm
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section B.2 FARMING — "Location of Farm"

fields.name: rentalPropertyAddress
fields.label: Address of Property (Rental Income)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.3 RENTAL INCOME — "Address of Property"

fields.name: employmentRemunerationEmployerName
fields.label: Employer Name (Remuneration)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.4(a) EMPLOYMENT Remuneration — "Name of Employer"

fields.name: employmentRemunerationPeriodEmployed
fields.label: Period Employed
fields.description: Modelled as free text: the form prints a single "Period Employed" column header with no separate From/To sub-labels (unlike Section B.3's Rental Income, which does print "Period Let From/To").

fields.name: employmentBenefitsEmployerName
fields.label: Employer Name (Benefits and Allowances)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.4(b) EMPLOYMENT Benefits and Allowances — "Name of Employer"

fields.name: employmentBenefitsDescription
fields.label: Description of Benefits and Allowances granted by the employer
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.4(b) — "Description of Benefits and Allowances granted by the employer"

fields.name: interestBuildingSocietyPayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer — Building Society Interest
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.5.a INTEREST — "Building Society" / "Name of Payer"

fields.name: interestBankPayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer — Bank Interest
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.5.b INTEREST — "Bank" / "Name of Payer"

fields.name: interestOtherPayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer — Other Interest
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.5.c INTEREST — "Other" / "Name of Payer"

fields.name: propertyDisposalDescription
fields.label: Description of Property (Property Disposal)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.6 PROPERTY DISPOSALS (OTHER THAN GIFTED PROPERTY) — "Description of Property"

fields.name: otherIncomePensionAnnuityPayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer — Pension/Annuity
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.7.a OTHER INCOME — "Pension/Annuity" / "Name of Payer"

fields.name: otherIncomeLine2NatureOfIncome
fields.label: Nature of Income (Other Income, line b)
fields.description: Section B.7's own instruction ("specify nature of income excluding dividends, see Guide") applies: unlike line a (fixed as "Pension/Annuity"), this second blank row ("b.") carries no printed nature, so the filer supplies it.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.7.b OTHER INCOME — "Nature of Income"

fields.name: otherIncomeLine2PayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer (Other Income, line b)

fields.name: otherIncomeLine3NatureOfIncome
fields.label: Nature of Income (Other Income, line c)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.7.c OTHER INCOME — "Nature of Income"

fields.name: otherIncomeLine3PayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer (Other Income, line c)

fields.name: foreignInvestmentInterestGrossIncome
fields.label: Gross Income — Foreign Investment Interest
fields.description: Section B.8 is explicitly scoped "(for Botswana citizens only)" on the form itself; not gated to countryOfCitizenship since that field is free text, not a Botswana/non-Botswana boolean.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.8 INCOME FROM FOREIGN INVESTMENTS (for Botswana citizens only) — row "Interest", "Gross Income"

fields.name: foreignInvestmentInterestAmountBroughtIntoBotswana
fields.label: Amount brought into Botswana — Foreign Investment Interest
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.8 — row "Interest", "Amount brought into Botswana"

fields.name: foreignInvestmentDividendsAmountBroughtIntoBotswana
fields.label: Amount brought into Botswana — Foreign Investment Dividends
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.8 — row "Dividends", "Amount brought into Botswana"

fields.name: foreignInvestmentBusinessAmountBroughtIntoBotswana
fields.label: Amount brought into Botswana — Foreign Investment Business
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section B.8 — row "Business", "Amount brought into Botswana"

fields.name: exemptIncomePayerName
fields.label: Name of Payer (Exempt Income)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section C GROSS INCOME CLAIMED TO BE EXEMPT FROM TAX — "Name of Payer"

fields.name: pensionFundEmployerName
fields.label: Name of Employer (Pension Fund Contributions)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section D.1 CONTRIBUTIONS TO AN APPROVED PENSION FUND — "Name of Employer"

fields.name: pensionFundContributionsInTaxYear
fields.label: Contributions made in the Tax Year
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section D.1 — "Contributions made in the Tax Year: P..."

fields.name: retirementAnnuityInsurerName
fields.label: Name of Insurer (Retirement Annuity Scheme)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section D.2 PREMIUMS PAID TO AN APPROVED RETIREMENT ANNUITY SCHEME — "Name of Insurer"

fields.name: retirementAnnuityPremiumsPaidInTaxYear
fields.label: Premiums paid in the Tax Year
fields.description: The form directs the filer to "Please enclose receipt" — the receipt itself is a supporting document outside this schema's field-level scope.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section D.2 — "Premiums paid in the Tax Year P... (Please enclose receipt)"

fields.name: taxCreditEmployerOrIncomeSourceName
fields.label: Name of Employer or Income Source (Tax Credits)
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section E TAX CREDITS — INCOME TAX DEDUCTED — "Name of Employer or Income Source"

fields.name: taxCreditAmountOfTax
fields.label: Amount of Tax (Tax Credits)
fields.description: The form directs the filer to enclose certificates or other evidence of tax withheld/paid, and notes credit is not available for tax withheld on dividends — both are sourcing/supporting-evidence notes, not further fields.

fields.name: usedRentedPropertyInTaxYear
fields.label: Did you use rented property for residential or business purposes in the tax year?
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section F GENERAL INFORMATION, Q1 — "Did you use rented property for residential or business purposes in the tax year?"

fields.name: rentedPropertyDetails
fields.label: Rented property details (address, landlord, rent, premium, annual rental)
fields.description: Collapses the form's own multi-part instruction into one free-text field: "state in the case of each property its address, the name and address of the landlord, the rent paid in the year, any premium paid in the year and the annual rental" — the printed blank lines carry no further sub-labels of their own.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section F, Q1 follow-up blank lines

fields.name: movablePropertyTransferredByGift
fields.label: Was any movable property transferred to you during the year by gift, inheritance or similar disposition?
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section F, Q2 — "Was any movable property transferred to you during the year by gift, inheritance or similar disposition?"

fields.name: giftedPropertyTransferorNameAndAddress
fields.label: Name and Address of the Transferor
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section F, Q2 follow-up — "state for each case the Name and Address of the Transferor"

fields.name: declarantFullName
fields.label: Full Name of Declarant
fields.description: The declaration's own printed address blank ("...of...") repeats Section A's Address for Service of Notice within the sworn-declaration sentence; not modelled as a second field.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section G DECLARATION — "I......of...... FULL NAME"

fields.name: declarationSignature
fields.label: Signature
fields.description: The form states no explicit "return will not be accepted unless signed" warning (unlike some sibling registry schemas), but Section G's own heading requires the declaration "to be completed and signed by the Taxpayer or his authorised agent."

fields.name: signedByAuthorisedAgent
fields.label: Signed by an Authorised Agent (rather than the Taxpayer)
fields.description: Disclosed interpretation: the form prints "Taxpayer/Authorised Agent* *Delete as appropriate", a manual strike-through convention rather than a printed checkbox. Modelled as a directly-supplied boolean flag.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section G — "Taxpayer/Authorised Agent* *Delete as appropriate"

fields.name: authorisedAgentFullName
fields.label: Full name of Authorised Agent
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section G — "...if signed by an Agent enter his full name below"
